articles.title: IPL 2026 Final Faces Fresh Security Alert - Details Inside
articles.description:
The IPL 2026 final has received an additional layer of security attention after the league’s Anti-Corruption Unit (ACU) issued a warning regarding the use of smart wearable devices inside restricted stadium areas. Advanced gadgets such as smart glasses, smart goggles, and connected smartwatches have now been placed under the scanner due to concerns surrounding unauthorized communication during matches.

The development comes amid the league’s ongoing efforts to tighten integrity protocols and prevent any possible misuse of modern technology during one of cricket’s biggest tournaments. ACU has reportedly informed franchises and team officials that such devices could become a serious threat if used within restricted operational zones during live games.

READ MORE | Sunil Gavaskar's 3-Word Message To Vaibhav Sooryavanshi After He Touched His Feet

Why Smart Glasses And Wearables Have Become A Concern

Unlike traditional accessories, many modern wearable devices now come equipped with features such as live-streaming, messaging, calling, internet access, and video recording through mobile data or Wi-Fi connectivity. These capabilities have prompted the ACU to classify several smart gadgets as prohibited communication tools during matches.

According to a latest report published by the Indian Express, “According to sources, the ACSU flagged that these smart devices are capable of live streaming, sending and receiving text messages, and making audio and video calls through mobile data or Wi-Fi networks, raising concerns over unauthorized communication and potential misuse during matches. Under the IPL’s Player and Match Officials Area (PMOA) Minimum Standards, such devices have been classified as “Audio/Video Recording Devices” and “Communication Devices”.”

Reports have also suggested that certain technology companies have been actively promoting smart eyewear products among IPL players and support staff during the ongoing season. The ACU’s latest advisory is aimed at ensuring that these gadgets do not compromise the integrity of the competition.

READ MORE | Suspense Ends On Lionel Messi As Argentina Reveal FIFA World Cup 2026 Squad

What Is PMOA And Why Is It Important?

The Player and Match Officials Area (PMOA) refers to highly secure sections within a stadium where access is limited to authorized individuals such as players, coaches, umpires, match officials, and anti-corruption officers. Strict rules are enforced in these zones to prevent any unauthorized interaction or communication during games.

This season already witnessed one controversy involving communication devices when Rajasthan Royals manager Romi Bhinder was reportedly seen using a mobile phone in the dugout during a league-stage fixture. While managers are permitted to carry phones inside dressing rooms for emergency purposes, their use in dugout areas is prohibited under IPL guidelines.The BCCI subsequently penalized Bhinder for violating PMOA rules.

“With wearable technology becoming more sophisticated and concerns growing over covert communication methods, the ACSU’s latest crackdown underlines the league’s attempt to stay ahead of evolving integrity risks during one of world cricket’s most commercially significant tournaments,” sources told the media house.

The report further mentioned, “Teams were advised to ensure that net bowlers, logistics staff, throwdown specialists and other temporary personnel remained within approved access protocols and did not carry unauthorized communication devices into sensitive areas.”

articles.title: WFI Appeals To Supreme Court To Stop Vinesh Phogat From Entering Asian Games Trials
articles.description:
The Wrestling Federation of India has launched an urgent legal challenge in the Supreme Court against a recent judicial directive allowing Olympian Vinesh Phogat to contest the upcoming Asian Games trials. The national sporting body claims the previous ruling is unlawful, asserting that the judiciary has overstepped its boundaries by interfering with strict internal eligibility criteria.

The Critical Legal Basis of the Appeal

In its Special Leave Petition, the federation officially labeled the high court order as completely illegal. The governing body argued it was denied an adequate opportunity to present a detailed response before the directive was issued.

ALSO READ | Harsh Dubey Dismisses Ravindra Jadeja Replacement Talks After Maiden India Call-Up

The administrative panel insists that inserting the athlete into the weekend trials directly violates established guidelines. They firmly believe that team selection for international multi-sport tournaments must remain the exclusive domain of national sports federations.

Anti-Doping Regulations Come In The Way

Providing crucial background to the ongoing dispute, the federation stated that the athlete had formally chosen to retire from professional wrestling back in December 2024. This was confirmed through official messages sent to the International Testing Agency.

According to global anti-doping regulations, any competitor returning from a period of retirement must undergo a mandatory six-month whereabouts tracking phase. This testing period must be completed before an individual becomes eligible to compete.

Unresolved Anti-Doping Procedures

The federation also highlighted a recorded missed drug test connected to an unsuccessful testing attempt on December 18, 2025. This incident resulted in a formal show-cause notice being issued on May 9.

ALSO READ | Yashasvi Jaiswal In MI? Opener Told To Leave Rajasthan Royals Over Vaibhav Sooryvanshi's Rise

The governing council maintains that this specific disciplinary procedure is still active. They argue the ongoing inquiry must be allowed to reach its natural conclusion before the athlete can return to the mat.

No Special Exemptions Permitted Under Current Guidelines

The official selection criteria published in February contains absolutely no provisions for iconic athlete status or maternity exemptions. The federation reiterates that squad selection has always been based strictly on performance and merit.

A total of twelve wrestlers have already qualified for the trials through standard domestic championship routes. The petition notes these compliant athletes were never given a fair hearing by the high court.

Pressing Accreditation Deadline Constraints

The national federation also submitted that the official accreditation deadline for the continental games had already expired on May 14. This logistical cutoff happened well before the high court issued its judgment.

The operational timing makes the implementation of the judicial instructions exceptionally difficult for tournament organisers. The entire sporting community now awaits the apex court's decision on this highly sensitive matter.
